meaning: n.哨兵线,警戒线,饰带vt.用警戒线围住
examples: Police formed a cordon between the two crowds.
I took a cordon bleu cookery course.
The miners forced their way through a police cordon.
The city doubled the cordon zone in 2007 and reduced the enforcement period by one hour.
At Michelin-starred Glass Hostaria, Cordon Bleu-trained chef Cristina Bowerman whips up a creative menu that changes frequently.
